text: AP Exam is May 9. Practice DBQ due Friday. Focus on Period 5 document analysis - industrialization and labor themes. See attached rubric.
Grade reports are due June 20. Make sure all assignments are submitted by June 17. No late work accepted after June 16 for full credit.
Reminder: Gettysburg field trip is May 8-9. Permission slips and $18 payment due by April 30. See History Club Google Classroom for details.
Unit 6 DBQ is due next Friday. Topic: Should the federal government have regulated industrial working conditions in the Gilded Age? 7 documents provided. Use HAPP analysis.
SAQ Quiz on Unit 7 (WWI and WWII) is Wednesday. Three short-answer questions, 25 minutes. Focus on causation and continuity and change over time thinking skills.
Period 3 only: You may revise your Unit 4 LEQ for up to half credit back. Revisions due by Friday. See my feedback comments in the rubric for what to improve.
20th Century History: Starting the Civil Rights Movement unit Monday. Read pp. 612-640 in the textbook this weekend. Primary source packet is attached.
AP US History: College Board released updated scoring guidelines for the LEQ. Posted them in the Unit 9 folder. Worth reviewing before the practice exam next month.
Period 5: Great work on the Triangle Shirtwaist DBQs overall. Grades and comments returned. Several of you scored 5/7 - strong document use. Common issue: not enough outside evidence.
Period 7: Unit 8 Cold War test is May 15. Multiple choice 40 questions, 2 SAQs, and 1 LEQ. Study guide is posted. Review Containment, Truman Doctrine, and Korean War primary sources.
Reminder to all APUSH sections: AP Exam fee waiver deadline is February 1. If you qualify for free/reduced lunch, apply with Mrs. Perez in the main office.
Period 3: I posted a DBQ graphic organizer template in the Exam Prep folder. This is the same format used on the actual AP Exam. Practice using it before the mock exam.
20th Century History: Boston history connection - the 1919 Boston Police Strike was one of the most significant labor events in the US. Primary sources posted. We discuss it Friday.
Period 5: The labor history unit primary source packet is now on Drive. Includes Triangle Shirtwaist fire documents, Lawrence Mill Strike accounts, and AFL-CIO statements.
Period 7: DBQ on US foreign policy 1890-1920 scores returned. Three students scored 7/7. Common issue was missing the complexity point. I posted a sample complexity paragraph in the materials.
Practice AP Exam is April 18 in the Eastbridge auditorium, 8:00am-12:30pm. Attendance required for all APUSH sections. Bring 2 pencils, 2 pens, and a photo ID.
Period 3: Parent-teacher conferences are May 20, 3:30-6pm. Sign up link is in Google Classroom. I especially want to speak with families of students borderline on the AP Exam score.
20th Century History: End of year reflection essay is due June 15. 3-4 pages. Choose one theme from our year and analyze how it shaped the modern United States.
Period 7: Complete the AP Exam survey in Google Forms by Monday. This helps me understand which periods and skills to focus on in our final review sessions.
Period 5: Very proud of how the class handled the Triangle Shirtwaist Socratic seminar today. Real historical empathy on display. Excellent work connecting to present-day labor issues.
